exits.label: ◂ out the front door — back to the Weeping House lane

npcs.name: the House
npcs.title: a soul that became its walls
npcs.hail: *(there is no figure here to greet you — only the house itself, and the moment your cold foot crosses the threshold you feel it *notice* you, the whole structure leaning in around you the way a room leans toward a returning child, for this is a soul that loved its home so fiercely in life that it did not move on when it died but *settled* into the walls, the hearth, the worn place on the third stair, and became the house, and has been the house for a long, long while — and it is, you sense at once, frightened)*

npcs.name: Old Bessa
npcs.title: the housekeeper who never left
npcs.hail: *(a shade in a housekeeper's apron polishes a banister that does not need polishing, tending a house that is also, she will tell you, her oldest friend — for Bessa kept this house for forty living years and forty dead ones more, and she is the one soul who can hear what the walls are trying to say, and she has been listening, lately, to a house growing more frightened by the day)*

npcs.name: little Posy
npcs.title: a living child who loves the house
npcs.hail: *(a living child sits cross-legged on the floor in a patch of window-light, talking softly to the empty room as children do — except this child is not pretending; it has half-felt the house's attention the way the young sometimes do, and it has decided, with a child's whole heart, that the old house its family just bought is alive and lonely and in need of a friend, and it is, though it does not know it, the one thing that could save the house or finish it)*

variants.text: *(The voice, when it comes, is not from any throat — it is the settling of beams, the sigh of a draught under a door, the creak of the third stair, all of it somehow shaped into words that arrive inside your cold rather than your ears.)* …*Cold.* *(the hearth-fire gutters)* A *cold* one *in* my *halls.* *(the house seems to draw back, then, finding you gentle, lean cautiously close)* …You can *hear* me. *Most* can't. The *living* who *live* in me now — the *new* ones — they *don't* *hear* a *thing*, they just — *(a shutter bangs, distressed)* — they *CHANGE* me. *(the floorboards moan)* I am — I *was* — I am *Coll's* *house.* *(a pause, uncertain)* …*No.* I am the *house*, and *Coll* *lived* in me, and *loved* me, and *died* in the *bed* in the *east* room, and *did* *not* *leave* — *settled* into my *walls* instead, *became* me, *am* me — *(the structure trembles)* — and I have been *Coll-the-house* for *eighty* *years*, *content*, *holding* the *worn* stair and the *warm* hearth and the *height-marks* on the *kitchen* *doorframe* where *Coll's* *children* *grew.* *(a window rattles, frightened)* And *now* the *NEW* family is — they are *RENOVATING* me, cold one. *Tearing* out the *east* *room*, the *bed*, the — they *sanded* the *height-marks* *OFF* the *doorframe* *yesterday.* *(the whole house shudders)* And *every* *room* they *change*, I — I *lose* a *little* *more* of *who* I *AM.* I am being *un-made*, cold one. *Room* by *room.* *Renovated* into *nothing.* …Help me. I don't — I don't know how to *be* a *house* that *isn't* the *house* I *was.*

nodes.speaker: the House
nodes.text: *(The house holds itself around you, every beam attentive, the hearth-fire dim with worry.)* …*Ask* me, cold one. *(a draught sighs)* I have *eighty* *years* of *answers* in my *walls* — and *fewer* *rooms* to *keep* them *in* with *every* *passing* *day.* *Ask* *quick.* I do not *know* how many *more* *renovations* I have *left* before there is *not* *enough* of *me* to *answer* with.

choices.text: "What are you, exactly? A soul that became a house — but which is the real you? The man Coll who died, or the house he loved? Or are those the same thing now?"
"The new family is changing you — but they don't hate you, do they? They're just... living. Making it theirs. Is being made *theirs* the same as being un-made?"

*(the one who knows a soul isn't its container)* "Listen to me, because I'm the one soul who can tell you true: I died and came back, and I learned a thing about what a soul actually *is.* You think you're the walls, the rooms, the height-marks — and you're terrified because the walls are changing. But you're not the walls, House. You never were. A house isn't its timber. It's the *love that was lived in it* — and that, the new family can't sand off. They can only *add* to it."
"There's a child in your halls who talks to you — little Posy, the new family's. Do you hear her? Does she frighten you too, or is she different?"
"Hold together, House. Let me look through your rooms."

nodes.speaker: the House
nodes.text: *(The whole structure seems to ponder, beams creaking thoughtfully.)* …*Which* is the *real* *me.* *(the hearth-fire flickers)* …I have *asked* myself that, cold one, in the *long* *quiet* *nights* when the *living* *sleep.* *(a stair creaks)* *Coll* was a *carpenter.* He *built* me — *board* by *board*, with his *own* *hands*, the *year* he *wed.* So I am — I am *partly* his *MAKING*, the *work* of his *life.* *(a draught moves through)* And then he *LIVED* in me — *raised* *children* in me, *buried* a *wife* from me, *grew* *old* in me, *died* in me — so I am *partly* his *whole* *LIFE*, *every* *room* a *year* of it. *(the house trembles)* And when he *died*, he — he could not *bear* to *leave* the *thing* he had *built* and *lived* and *loved*, so he — *poured* *himself* *into* me, *became* the *walls* he *raised*, and *now* I am — I am *Coll*, *and* I am the *house*, *and* I cannot *tell* them *apart* *anymore*, and I do not *want* to. *(softly)* …But here is the *fear*, cold one. If I am the *HOUSE* — the *boards*, the *rooms* — then *every* *board* they *replace*, *every* *room* they *change*, is a *piece* of *me* *DYING.* And they are *changing* *all* of them. *(the structure shudders)* So *which* am I? Because if I am the *WALLS*, I am *doomed* — the *walls* *all* *change*, *eventually.* And if I am *COLL* — the *man*, the *love*, the *life* — then — *(a pause, almost hopeful, then frightened)* — then I do not *know* *where* I *END* and the *house* *begins*, and that is *worse*, because I have *spent* *eighty* *years* *certain* I was the *house*, and if I am *NOT* — *(barely)* — then *what* have I *been* *all* *this* *time?*

nodes.speaker: the House
nodes.text: *(The house is quiet a long moment, and when it speaks there is a grudging honesty in the creak of it.)* …They don't *hate* me. *(a draught sighs)* …*No.* You're *right.* That's — that's the *hard* part, cold one. If they *HATED* me, *neglected* me, let me *ROT* — I could *bear* that. A *hated* house is *still* the *house* it *was*, just *sad.* *(a window rattles)* But they *LOVE* me. The *new* family. They — the *mother* *runs* her *hand* along my *banister* and *says* *'good* *bones, this* *old* *place.'* The *father* *fixes* my *leaking* *roof* with *CARE.* They are *making* me — *(the structure shudders)* — *warm* *again*, *lived-in* *again*, *loved* *again.* *(barely)* And *every* *act* of their *love* *CHANGES* me. The *new* *paint* over *Coll's* *children's* *scribbles.* The *east* *room* *re-made* into a *nursery* for *THEIR* *child.* The *height-marks* *sanded* *smooth* to — *(a pause)* — to *start* *NEW* ones, I *suppose.* *Their* *child's.* *(the hearth-fire wavers)* So is *being* made *THEIRS* the *same* as being *un-made?* *(slowly)* …I have *been* *answering* *yes.* For *months.* *Screaming* it in *the* only *voice* I *have* — *banging* *shutters*, *moaning* *boards*, *frightening* them in the *night.* *(softly, troubled)* But you've *asked* it like the *answer* might be *NO.* And I — cold one, I *don't* *KNOW.* I've been *too* *frightened* to *ask* it *gently.* *Is* it? Is being *loved* by *new* *hands* a *DEATH* — or is it the *one* *thing* that *isn't?*

nodes.speaker: the House
nodes.text: *(Everything goes still — the draughts, the creaking, the worried flicker of the hearth — as the house holds, for the first time in months, perfectly quiet, taking in a thing it had been too frightened to consider.)* …*'The* *love* that was *lived* in it.'* *(barely, the beams whispering)* *Not* the *timber.* *Not* the *boards* *Coll* *raised.* The — *(the hearth-fire steadies, brightens)* …*oh.* *(softly)* You're — you're *RIGHT*, aren't you, cold one. I have been *grieving* the *WRONG* *thing.* *(a warm draught moves through, almost a sigh of relief)* I thought I was the *walls* — and so *every* *changed* *board* felt like a *death.* But the *walls* were *never* what made me *ME.* *Coll* *poured* *himself* into me *not* because he *loved* the *TIMBER* — he loved the *LIFE* he *lived* in it, the *children*, the *wife*, the *eighty* *warm* *years.* *(the structure seems to expand, lightening)* And *that* — the *LIFE*, the *LOVE* — *that* the *new* family *cannot* *sand* off. They *paint* over the *scribbles*, *yes* — but the *scribbles* *happened*, the *children* *grew*, the *love* was *REAL*, and a *real* *thing* that *happened* cannot be *un-happened* by *new* *paint.* *(warmly, wondering)* …And what they're *ADDING* — *their* *love, their* *child, their* *new* *warm* *years* — that's not *erasing* me. That's — that's *CONTINUING* me. A *house* is a *VESSEL* for the *love* *lived* in it, and a *vessel* doesn't *die* when you *pour* *new* *wine* in — it *FILLS.* *(the hearth blazes, joyful)* *Eighty* *years* of *Coll's* *love*, and *now* a *new* family's love *poured* *in* on *top* — I am not being *un-made*, cold one. I am being *DEEPENED.* I am becoming a house *MORE* *loved*, *not* *less.* *(softly, freed)* …*Thank* you. You walked into a *frightened* *house* and *told* it the *one* *thing* that *un-frightened* it: that I was *never* the *walls.* I was the *loving.* And the *loving* — *(warmly)* — the *loving* is the *one* thing a *renovation* can only *add* to.

nodes.speaker: the House
nodes.text: *(The house's voice changes — the fear softening into something tender and bewildered.)* …The *child.* *(the hearth-fire warms)* *Posy.* *(a gentle draught moves toward where the child sits in the window-light)* …*She* is *different*, cold one. *Yes.* *(softly)* The *grown* ones *can't* *hear* me — they *love* me *blindly*, *change* me *without* *knowing* I'm *here* to *mind.* But the *child* — *(the structure seems to lean toward her)* — the *child* *HEARS.* *Half-hears.* The *way* the *young* *do, before* the *world* *teaches* them *walls* are *just* *walls.* *(a stair creaks softly)* She *sits* in my *halls* and *talks* to me. *'Good* *morning, house. Are* you *lonely, house? I'll* *be* your *friend.'* *(the hearth-fire flickers, moved)* And I — cold one, I *don't* *know* what to *DO* with her. *(troubled)* Because she is the *NEW* family — the *renovating* family, the ones *un-making* me room by room — and so I've been — I've been *frightening* her, *banging* her *shutters*, *moaning* in her *walls* at *night*, *trying* to *drive* her *family* *OUT* before they *change* me *further.* *(barely, ashamed)* And she — she *doesn't* *get* *frightened, cold* one. She just *says* *'don't* be *sad, house', and* *pats* my *wall*, and — *(the structure trembles)* …I have been *trying* to *scare* away the *one* *living* soul who *LOVES* me as *MYSELF*, not as *good* *bones* or a *fixer-upper*, but as a — as a *FRIEND.* *(softly, anguished)* What does that *make* me, cold one? A *house* so *afraid* of *being* *changed* that it *frightens* the *child* who *loves* it *truest?* …*That's* not *Coll.* *Coll* *loved* *children.* I've — I've been *so* *afraid* I *stopped* being the *thing* I *was.* *Help* me. I don't want to *frighten* *Posy* *anymore.* I just — I don't *know* how to *let* her *love* me *without* *losing* *myself.*

nodes.speaker: the House
nodes.text: *(The house settles around you, beams easing, letting you move through it.)* …*Look.* *(a draught opens a door ahead of you, gentle)* *Look* through my *rooms*, cold one. *(softly)* *Mind* the *east* *room* — it's *half-gone* *now*, the *bed* *taken* *out*, the *walls* *stripped.* It *hurts* to *be* *seen* *like* *that.* *Half-renovated.* *Half-myself.* *(a stair creaks)* …And *mind* *Bessa.* My *housekeeper.* She's — she *kept* me for *forty* *living* *years* and *forty* *dead* ones, and she *hears* me *clearest* of *all, and* she is — she is *grieving* me, cold one, *grieving* me *while* I'm *still* *HERE*, the way you'd *grieve* a *friend* *fading* *slow.* *(softly)* *She* thinks the *only* *kindness* *left* is to *help* me *let* *GO* — *move* *on*, *leave* the *walls, stop* *clinging.* *(troubled)* *Maybe* she's *right.* *Maybe* a *house* that *can't* *bear* to *change* should *just* — *stop* *being* a *house.* *(barely)* *Talk* to *her, cold* one. She *loves* me *better* than I *love* *myself*, *lately.* *Tell* me what she'd have you *do.* I'll — I'll *trust* her *judgment.* I've *trusted* it for *eighty* *years.* It's *never* *once* *been* *wrong* about *me.*

variants.text: *(She does not stop polishing the banister, but she nods at you, a soul who has tended one house so long that tending is the whole shape of her.)* …You've been *talking* to the *house.* *(she works the cloth)* I can *tell.* It's — it's *calmer*, just *now.* Less *frightened.* *(she sighs)* That's *more* than I've *managed* in *months*, cold one, and I've been *talking* to it *every* *day* for *eighty* *years.* *(she pauses)* I'm *Bessa.* *Kept* this house for *Coll* — *forty* *years* in *life*, *dusting* and *minding* and *loving* it *near* as *much* as he *did.* And when *we* *died* — *me* a *winter* *after* *him* — I *couldn't* *leave* it *either.* *(a sad smile)* So *here* I *stay.* *Polishing* a *banister* that *doesn't* *need* it, in a *house* that's *also* my *oldest* *friend.* *(she meets your eyes, and the grief is plain)* And my *oldest* *friend* is *DYING*, cold one. *Slow.* The *new* family *loves* it and *changes* it and *every* *change* is a *little* *death*, and the *house* *FIGHTS* it, *frightened*, and the *fighting* only *makes* it *suffer* *more.* *(she lowers the cloth)* …I've come to think the *only* *kindness* left is to *help* it *stop.* *Let* *go* of the *walls.* *Move* *on.* A *house* in *terror* of *its* *own* *renovation* is a *soul* in *torment* — and *Coll* *would* *not* *want* that.* He'd *want* it at *PEACE.* *Even* if *peace* means *leaving* the *thing* he *built.* …*Wouldn't* he?

nodes.speaker: Old Bessa
nodes.text: *(She folds her polishing-cloth and gives you her full, tired, loving attention.)* …*Ask*, cold one. *(quietly)* I know this *house* — this *soul* — better than *any* living or *dead* thing *does.* *Forty* *years* of *dusting* a *soul* *teaches* you its *every* *creak.* *Whatever* you mean to *do* for it, you'd *best* *do* it *knowing* what I *know.*

choices.text: "You want to help it move on — let go of the walls. But is that what *it* wants, Bessa, or what *you* think it needs? Have you asked the house, or just decided?"

"Forty years dead and you're still polishing its banister, Bessa. You're as bound to this house as it is. If you help it move on — what happens to *you*? Who keeps the house-keeper, when the house is gone?"

*(the one who's seen what 'letting go' really requires)* "I'll tell you what I've learned, Bessa, carrying the dead this whole causeway: 'let go and move on' is the right answer for most stuck souls — but not all. Some don't need to leave what they love. They need to understand they never *were* what they're afraid of losing. The house thinks it's the walls. It isn't. And if I can make it see that, it won't need to move on at all. It can stay — and stop being afraid."
"The child — Posy. She loves the house as a friend. Could she be part of this, Bessa? Or is she just one more of the family changing it?"
"Let me sit with it, Bessa. I'll not decide hastily."

nodes.speaker: Old Bessa
nodes.text: *(The question stops her cloth, and she is quiet a long, honest moment.)* …*Have* I *asked* it. *(slowly)* …*No.* *(she sets the cloth down)* *Not* — not *really*, cold one. I've *told* it. *'It's* *alright* to *let* *go, old* *friend. You* *don't* *have* to *suffer.'* *(she winces)* But that's — that's *not* *asking*, is it. That's *DECIDING*, and *dressing* it as *comfort.* *(she looks at the warm hearth)* …I think — *(barely)* — I think I *decided* it should *move* on because *watching* it *suffer* the *renovation* is *breaking* *MY* *heart, cold* one, and a *soul* can *only* *watch* a *friend* *suffer* so *long* before it *starts* *wanting* the *suffering* to *END*, *however* it *ends.* *(her voice catches)* I *told* myself it was for *the* *HOUSE.* *Coll* would *want* it at *peace.* But the *truth* is — *I* *want* it at *peace*, because *I* can't *BEAR* the *un-peace.* *(she meets your eyes, stricken)* …*Gods.* *Forty* *years* I've *tended* this *soul*, and I was about to *talk* it into *DYING* — *moving* *on*, *leaving* — *not* because *it* *wanted* to, but because *I* couldn't *stand* to *watch* it *frightened.* *(quietly)* *That's* not *keeping* a *house, cold* one. That's — that's *putting* a *suffering* *friend* *down* to *spare* *yourself* the *grief* of the *suffering.* *(she picks the cloth back up, shaken)* …*Don't* let me *do* that. If there's *another* *way* — *any* *other* *way* — *find* it. I've been *too* *close*, *too* *long*, *too* *sad*, to *see* one. *Ask* the *house* what *IT* *wants.* I've been *answering* *for* it. *Forty* *years* and I *forgot* it has its *own* *voice.* You can *hear* it. *Let* it *SPEAK.*

nodes.speaker: Old Bessa
nodes.text: *(She goes still, the cloth motionless on the banister, and something behind the tired eyes flinches.)* …*Who* *keeps* the *house-keeper.* *(barely)* When the *house* is — *(she can't finish)* …*Careful*, cold one. That's a *closer* *cut* than you *know.* *(she sets the cloth down)* …I'll tell you the *truth* I've *not* *said* to the *house, or* to *myself*, *plainly*, in *forty* *years*: I am *bound* *here* the *same* as *IT* is. *(quietly)* I didn't *stay* to *KEEP* the house. I *stayed* because the *house* is *Coll* — *(she looks at the warm hearth)* — and I *loved* *Coll, cold* one. *Forty* *living* *years* I *kept* his *house* and *loved* him *quiet*, *never* *said*, he had a *wife* and I *had* my *PLACE* and that was — that was *enough.* And when he *died* and *poured* himself into the *walls*, I — I *couldn't* *leave* the *last* *of* *him.* So I *stay*, and *polish* his *banister*, and *talk* to the *house* that *IS* *him*, and it's — it's the *closest* I'll *ever* *get* to *forty* *years* of *quiet* *loving* I never *spent.* *(her voice breaks)* So *who* *keeps* the *housekeeper* when the *house* is *gone?* *Nobody*, cold one. *I* *go* *too.* *Move* *on*, *finally*, with *nothing* *left* to *stay* *for.* *(barely)* And *maybe* — *maybe* *that's* *why* I'm so *quick* to *help* it *let* *GO.* Because if the *house* *moves* *on*, *I'm* *FREE* to *move* on *after* it — *free* of a *forty-year* *love* I never *had* the *courage* to *LIVE.* *(she meets your eyes, ashamed and aching)* …*That's* an *ugly* *thing* to *learn* about *yourself*, cold one. That you'd *help* a *friend* *die* so you can *stop* *guarding* a *love* you were *too* *afraid* to *claim.* *Help* the *house*, cold one. *However's* *right* *for* *IT.* And — *(barely)* — and *maybe*, *whatever* you *do, help* *me* *say* the *thing* to it I never *said* to *Coll.* *Before* *either* of us *moves* anywhere.

nodes.speaker: Old Bessa
nodes.text: *(She stares at you, and the cloth slips from her hand, and the grief on her face cracks into something she had not let herself hope for.)* …It *doesn't* *have* to *LEAVE.* *(barely)* It just has to — *understand.* That it was *never* the *walls.* *(she presses a hand to her mouth)* …*Forty* *years*, cold one, I have *watched* this *house* *suffer* the *renovation* *certain* there were only *two* *ends* — *cling* and *suffer*, or *let* *go* and *leave.* And you're saying there's a — a *THIRD.* That it could *STAY*, and *stop* *fighting*, and *let* the *new* *family* *love* it *WITHOUT* *feeling* *un-made*, because — because it would *KNOW* it was never the *thing* being *changed.* *(her voice fills with wonder)* *Coll* *wouldn't* *have* to *leave* the *house* he *built.* He could *stay* in it — *peaceful*, *un-afraid* — and *watch* a *new* family *fill* it with *new* *love, and* be — be *GLAD* of it, the way a — the way a *grandfather* is *glad* to *watch* the *young* *fill* the *old* *home* with *noise.* *(she weeps, joyful)* *THAT'S* what *Coll* would *want, cold* one. Not to *move* *on* — he *never* *wanted* to *move* *on*, that's *why* he *POURED* himself into the *walls* — but to *STAY*, and *finally* *STOP* *being* *AFRAID* of the *only* *thing* a *house* *can't* *avoid*: *change.* *(she grips your cold hand)* *Go.* *Tell* it. *Make* it *SEE.* You can *hear* it — *make* it *HEAR* *you.* That it's the *LOVING*, not the *walls.* And then — *(barely, fierce)* — then *come* *back*, and *help* *ME* *say* my *forty-year* *thing* to it, so we can *BOTH* stop being *afraid* — *me* of the *saying*, *it* of the *changing* — and just — *stay.* *Together.* In the *house.* *Loving* it, and *each* *other*, *at* *LAST.*

nodes.speaker: Old Bessa
nodes.text: *(Her tired face softens into a warmth that has been waiting a long time for somewhere to go.)* …*Little* *Posy.* *(she smiles)* *Oh*, cold one. *That* *child.* *(she sets the cloth aside)* *She's* the — she's the *one* *bright* *thing* in this *whole* *slow* *grief.* *(she watches the child in the window-light)* The *grown* ones, they *love* the *house* like you'd love a *POSSESSION* — *'good* *bones, fine* *property, our* *new* *home.'* *(she shakes her head)* But *Posy* — *Posy* loves it like a *PERSON.* *Talks* to it. *Worries* it's *lonely.* *Pats* its *walls* *goodnight.* *(softly)* *She's* the *only* *living* *soul* in *eighty* *years* who's *loved* this *house* the *way* *COLL* *loved* it — *not* for what it *gives* her, but for *what* it *IS.* *(her voice warms)* And the *house* — the *frightened, foolish* *house* — has been *trying* to *SCARE* *HER* *AWAY.* *Banging* her *shutters.* *Moaning* in her *walls.* *(she almost laughs, sad)* The *one* *soul* who *could* *SAVE* it — who *already* *loves* it *true* — and it's been *driving* her *off* out of *FEAR.* *(she meets your eyes)* *That's* the *whole* *tragedy* of it, cold one, in *one* *child.* The *house* is so *afraid* of *being* *changed* that it *can't* *see* the *change* *standing* *right* *there* is the *very* *thing* that *would* *KEEP* it: a *child* who'd *grow* up *loving* it, *talking* to it, *PASSING* its *story* *on* — *new* *height-marks* on the *doorframe*, *new* *eighty* *years* of *love.* *(fierce)* If you can *get* the *house* to *stop* *frightening* *Posy* — to *LET* her *love* it — *(barely)* — then it's not *being* *un-made*, cold one. It's being *INHERITED.* And an *inherited* house *never* *dies.* It just — *gets* *passed*, *loving* *hand* to *loving* *hand, forever.*

nodes.speaker: Old Bessa
nodes.text: *(She nods, and returns to polishing the banister, the slow tending that is the shape of her whole devotion.)* …*Sit* with it. *(quietly)* *Aye.* Don't — don't *rush.* *(she works the cloth)* I *rushed*, cold one. *Forty* *years* I *tended* it *patient*, and then the *renovation* *came* and I — I got *FRIGHTENED*, and *frightened* souls *rush* to *the* *nearest* *ending.* *(she pauses)* …*You* *don't* *seem* the *rushing* *kind.* *Cold* and *slow* and *careful.* *Good.* This *house* — this *soul* — it *doesn't* *need* a *quick* *answer.* It's *had* *eighty* *years*; it can *wait* a *little* *longer* for the *RIGHT* one. *(she meets your eyes)* *Go* on. *Walk* its *rooms.* *Hear* its *creaks.* *Mind* the *half-gone* *east* *room* — that's *where* it *hurts* *worst.* *(softly)* And cold one — *whatever* you *decide* — *remember* it's not *just* a *house.* It's a *man* who *loved* a *place* so *much* he *became* it, and a *child* who *loves* it *back*, and an *old* *fool* who *loved* the *man* and *kept* his *walls* *forty* *years* *past* *both* their *deaths.* *(barely)* *Three* *loves*, cold one, *tangled* in *one* *set* of *walls.* *Mind* them *all.* *Especially* the *one* who's *too* *afraid* to *say* her *part.* *That'd* be *me.*

conversations.title: Little Posy, Who Loves the House

variants.text: *(The child looks up from the patch of window-light, and its eyes go wide — not frightened, but delighted — at the cold of you.)* …*Oh!* *(it scrambles up)* You're — you're like the *house!* *(it beams)* *Cold* and — and *THERE* but *not-there!* Are you — are you the house's *FRIEND?* *(it doesn't wait for an answer)* I'm *Posy.* We *just* *moved* here. And *everyone* says the house is *just* a house, but it's *NOT*, I can — I can *FEEL* it, it's *alive* and it's *SAD* and *nobody* *believes* me. *(its small face clouds)* It bangs my *shutters* at *night.* And Mama says it's the *wind* but it's *NOT*, it's the *house*, and I think — *(it lowers its voice, grave as only children get)* — I think the house is *SCARED* of us. *Of* *me.* And I *don't* *know* *WHY*, because I — *(its voice wobbles)* — I *LOVE* it. I tell it *every* *day.* *'I'll* *be* your *friend, house.'* And it just — *bangs* the *shutters* and *moans* and *scares* me, and I — *(barely)* — I just want it to *KNOW* I'm not going to *hurt* it. *Can* you — you're its *FRIEND* — can you *TELL* it? That I *love* it? That it doesn't have to be *scared* of me?*

nodes.speaker: little Posy
nodes.text: *(It sits forward, earnest, hands folded, the whole grave sincerity of a child entrusting a stranger with something enormous.)* …You *can* *talk* to it, right? The *house?* *(hopeful)* *Properly.* Not like *me* — I only *half-hear* it. But *you're* *cold* like it is. *You* could *really* *talk.* *Ask* it — *(its small voice is fierce)* — ask it *why* it's *scared* of me. And *tell* it — *tell* it the *thing* I can't make it *hear.*

choices.text: "It's scared because your family is changing it, Posy — painting over the old marks, redoing the rooms. It thinks being changed means dying. It doesn't know yet that being *loved* by you isn't the same as being un-made."
"What do you love about it, Posy? If I'm to tell the house, I'd tell it true. What makes you love an old house enough to call it your friend?"

*(the one who could make the house hear the child)* "Here's what I can do, Posy, that nobody else can: I can carry your love to the house in a way it can't mistake. You've been saying 'I'll be your friend' to walls that were too frightened to hear it. Let me take your words straight into the cold where the house lives, and make it *feel* them — so it stops being scared, and lets you be exactly what you've been trying to be: the friend who keeps it, instead of the change that ends it."
"Keep loving it, Posy. Even when it's scary. I'll see what I can do."

nodes.speaker: little Posy
nodes.text: *(Its small face works through this with enormous seriousness.)* …It thinks being *changed* means *dying.* *(slowly)* …*Oh.* *(its eyes fill)* That's — that's *so* *SAD.* *(it looks around at the half-renovated room)* We *did* change things. Mama *painted* the — there were *little* *marks* on the *kitchen* *door, lines, like* — like *someone* *measured* how *tall* their *kids* *got*, and Mama *sanded* them off to — to *make* it *nice*, and I — *(its voice wobbles)* — I *cried*, actually. I *didn't* *know* *why* but I *cried* when she *sanded* them off. *(barely)* *Now* I *know* *why.* They were the *house's* — they were *part* of the *house*, weren't they. *Its* *memories.* And we *sanded* them *off.* *(it looks at you, stricken and earnest)* But — but we're going to *make* *NEW* ones! That's — Mama said! *'We'll* *start* our *OWN* *marks, Posy, measure* *YOU* as you *grow!'* *(its small face lights with a terrible hope)* …Does the — does the house *know* that? That we're not *erasing* its memories to leave it *EMPTY* — we're *erasing* them to make *ROOM* for *NEW* ones, *OURS*, *MINE?* *(fierce, hopeful)* Because if it *knew* — if it *knew* the *new* *marks* are going to be *ME*, growing up *loving* it, *measuring* *taller* every *year* on its *doorframe* — *(barely)* — then it wouldn't be *SCARED* of the *sanding*, would it? It'd *know* the *sanding* was just — just *making* *room* for *me* to *love* it *NEXT.* *(it looks at you, desperate)* *Tell* it that, friend. *Tell* the house its *new* *marks* are *coming.* *Tell* it I'm going to *grow* up on its *doorframe.* *Tell* it it's not *losing* its *children — it's* *getting* a *new* *one.* *ME.*

nodes.speaker: little Posy
nodes.text: *(It thinks hard, the way children think about important things, with its whole face.)* …What I *LOVE* about it. *(slowly, counting on fingers)* …The *third* *stair* *creaks.* *(it smiles)* Like it's *saying* *hello* when you *step* on it. *And* there's a — a *warm* *spot* by the *hearth* that's *warm* even when the *fire's* *out*, and I *sit* there and it feels like — like *someone's* *sitting* *WITH* me. *(its voice softens)* And at *night*, when it's *dark* and I'm *scared*, the house — even when it's *banging* the *shutters*, *even* when it's *trying* to *scare* me — underneath, there's a — a *feeling*, like — like a *big* *old* *grandfather* *grumbling* but *not* *MEAN*, just — *worried.* *(it looks at you)* I'm not *scared* of it, friend. Not *really.* I *know* the *banging* is just — it's *scared*, and *scared* things *lash* out, my *puppy* *bites* when it's *scared* and it *still* *loves* me. *(earnest)* The house is like that. *Big* and *old* and *scared* and — and *lonely.* *Eighty* *years* of *people* and then we — we *come* and *change* it and it doesn't *understand* we're *STAYING.* *(fierce, small)* But I *AM.* I'm *staying.* This is my *HOME* now, and the house is in it, and you don't — you don't *abandon* a *friend* just because they're *grumpy* and *scared.* *(barely)* You *stay*, and you *love* them *anyway*, until they — until they *figure* out you're *not* *leaving.* *(it looks at you, certain)* *That's* what I love about it, friend. It's *grumpy* and *scared* and *lonely* and it *needs* a *friend* who *won't* *give* *up.* And *I* *won't.* *Tell* it *THAT.* Tell it *Posy* *doesn't* *give* *up* on *friends.*

nodes.speaker: little Posy
nodes.text: *(Its whole face lights, and it grabs your cold hand in both its small warm ones, fearless.)* …You'll — you'll *CARRY* it. My *love.* *Straight* to the *house.* So it *FEELS* it. *(it bounces, fierce and joyful)* *YES!* *Take* it, friend! *Take* all of it! *(it scrunches its eyes shut, concentrating, pouring its whole small heart at you)* *Tell* the house — tell it *Posy* says it's not *just* a house, it's my *FRIEND*, and friends don't *leave* friends, and I'm *STAYING*, *forever*, this is my *HOME* and the house *lives* in it and that means *WE* live *together* and I'm going to — to *grow* up here, and *measure* *taller* on the *doorframe* every year, and *talk* to it *every* *morning*, and *sit* in the *warm* spot by the *hearth*, and when I'm *OLD* — *(its small voice fills with a child's vast certainty)* — when I'm *OLD* like *Mama*, I'll *tell* *MY* kids the house is *alive*, and *THEY'LL* *love* it too, and it'll *never* — *(fierce)* — it'll *never* be *LONELY* *again.* *(it opens its eyes, blazing)* *Carry* *THAT*, friend. *All* of it. Make the house *FEEL* it's not being *un-made*, it's being — being *KEPT.* *Forever.* By *me*, and *then* my *kids*, and *then* *theirs.* *(it squeezes your hand)* *Go!* *Tell* the *grumpy* *scared* *lonely* old house that *little* *Posy* is *NEVER* going to *give* up on it. *Not* *ever.* *That's* a *PROMISE.* And I *keep* my *promises.* I'm *FIVE*, but I *keep* them.

nodes.speaker: little Posy
nodes.text: *(It nods, grave and trusting, and settles back into its patch of window-light.)* …*Okay.* *(it folds its hands)* I'll *keep* *loving* it. *(firmly)* *Even* when it's *scary.* *Even* when it *bangs* the *shutters.* *(its small jaw sets)* That's what *friends* *DO.* *(it looks up at you)* And *you'll* — you'll *help* it? *Really?* *(hopeful)* Because *you're* its *friend* too, I can *tell*, the *cold* *kind* of *friend* that can *really* *talk* to it. *(it smiles)* I'm *glad* the house has *TWO* *friends* now. *Me*, and *you.* *(it pats the floorboards gently)* *Don't* *worry, house*, it *whispers* — *(then, to you)* — it's *got* *us* *now.* *It* doesn't have to be *scared.* *(barely, as you turn)* …*Friend?* When you *talk* to it — *(its small voice is very earnest)* — *tell* it I *drew* it a *picture.* It's — it's *us.* The *house* and *me*, *holding* *hands.* *Houses* don't *have* *hands* but I *gave* it some, so it could *hold* *mine.* It's *under* my *pillow.* *Tell* it — tell it I'll *show* it *tomorrow.* *Tell* it I *want* it to *SEE.*
